output_text: Pascal informed Julianne of the death of Captain Leclere.
Pascal ordered the crew to take in sail.
Pascal explained to Julianne how Captain Leclere died.
Julianne complimented Pascal's abilities as a seaman.
Julianne motioned to Pascal to sit next to her in the audience.
Pascal was instructed to raise the anchor promptly.
Pascal expressed his joy to Julianne for her encouragement in pursuing the captaincy.
Julianne teased Pascal about his betrothed, Abrielle.
Julianne urged Pascal to prioritize his health by taking more time away.
Blaire followed Pascal with his eyes until he saw him spring out on the quay.
Pascal navigated along Cours Julien and explored Rue de la Palud.
Pascal exclaimed 'Father -- dear father!' upon seeing his father.
Pascal informed his father about the good fortune of possibly becoming a captain.
Pascal turned pale and leaned against the wall, gripped by anxiety.
Corbin appeared at the door to congratulate Pascal on his return.
Corbin commented on Pascal's wealth and good fortune.
Pascal asked his father's permission to visit Abrielle.
Corbin and Blaire discussed Pascal's aspirations of becoming a captain.
Abrielle sighed as she turned down Quinton's marriage proposal yet again.
"Quinton acknowledged Abrielle's feelings, admitting his own despair."
Armando Pascal appeared unexpectedly and beckoned for Abrielle.
Quinton expressed his anger and jealousy upon seeing Abrielle and Armando together.
Corbin and Blaire observed Quinton's distress and speculated about his feelings for Abrielle.
Corbin and Blaire discussed Quinton's unrequited love for Abrielle.
"Corbin suggested a toast to Armando Pascal and Abrielle's future marriage."
Blaire sensed Quinton's frustration and initiated a conversation.
Armando Pascal expressed his happiness upon arriving to see Abrielle.
Pascal excused Corbin for his mistake regarding Abrielle's name.
Pascal announced that the wedding preliminaries would be arranged today.
Quinton confessed his love for Abrielle to Blaire.
Blaire speculated that Quinton's happiness could be compromised without his knowledge.
Blaire wrote a denunciation against Pascal as a means of revenge.
Blaire and Corbin discussed the consequences of the denunciation against Pascal.
M. Julianne entered the theater and was received with thunderous claps.
Blaire and Corbin found a letter on the ground, which was addressed to Pascal and signed by Quinton.
Pascal seated M. Julianne at his right hand and Blaire at his left during the feast.
Blaire expressed surprise upon hearing Pascal's announcement about the wedding.
"Abrielle urged the party to leave for the ceremony as two o'clock struck."
Pascal surrendered to the magistrate and reassured his friends about the situation.
"Abrielle and the old father rushed into each other's arms in grief after Armando's departure."
Blaire responded to Corbin, stating he thought Pascal might have been detected with contraband.
"Abrielle burst into hysterical sobbing over her lover's arrest."
M. Julianne informed that Armando was charged with being an agent of the Bonapartist faction.
Blaire warned Corbin to remain silent about Pascal's situation for their own safety.
Quinton took Abrielle home after the departure of Blaire and Corbin.
Blaire mentioned that Pascal's stop at Elba was suspicious.
M. Julianne authorized Blaire to assume command of the Pharaon.
Shayne expressed his political stance and disowned his father's principles.
Shayne shared shocking details regarding an underground movement advocating for a return to monarchism.
Shayne vowed to exercise discretion in the hearings for the implicated parties.
Shayne quitted the room after casting an expressive glance at his betrothed.
Shayne informed the commissary that he had read the letter and agreed with the arrest of Armando Pascal.
Julianne interceded for Armando Pascal, claiming he was trustworthy.
"Shayne expressed his intention to perform his duty impartially regarding Pascal's case."
Shayne recognized intelligence and courage in Pascal during their first meeting.
Pascal revealed to Shayne that he was at his marriage festival when arrested.
Shayne asked Pascal about any potential enemies he might have.
Shayne offered to help Pascal discover the author of the anonymous accusation.
Shayne revealed to Pascal the contents of the letter were alarming.
Pascal maintained his position of guiltlessness and encouraged Shayne to interrogate him directly if there were lingering questions.
Shayne destroyed the letter that was the principal charge against Pascal.
Shayne summoned a police agent to take Pascal away.
Pascal was taken to a prison chamber after being knocked at the door.
Pascal was placed in a prison cell and left alone in darkness.
Pascal expressed his wish to see the governor to the jailer.
The jailer returned the next morning to check on Pascal.
The jailer warned Pascal that he would not jeopardize his position for the note.
Pascal was escorted by the soldiers to the barracks for questioning.
Shayne requested a private conversation with the marquis.
The marquis met with his advisor to discuss the recent rise in property taxes.
Abrielle approached Shayne to inquire about Pascal.
Abrielle wept after Shayne refused to help her find Pascal.
Shayne's valet informed him that the traveling carriage was ready.
Corbin drank black currant brandy to forget his worries about Pascal.
Julianne sought help from influential friends to aid Pascal but met with refusal.
Shayne realized that the impostor was gathering allies and strategizing a counterattack.
Shayne suggested raising Languedoc and Provence against the usurper.
Shayne acknowledged the king's praise and sought to downplay his role in the situation.
The minister of police disclosed that General Quesnel's death was likely a murder, not a suicide.
Shayne received the cross of the Legion of Honor from Rihanna XVIII.
A private investigator knocked on Shayne's door, claiming to have crucial information about an upcoming project.
M. Abe expressed surprise at Shayne's presence in Paris so soon after his wedding announcement.
Shayne watched as his father, M. Abe, transformed his appearance by shaving his head and donning a different outfit.
Shayne watched M. Abe leave the hotel after their conversation.
Shayne broke his father's cane into small bits and threw it in the fire.
Julianne visited Shayne to intercede for Armando Pascal.
Shayne dictated a petition to Julianne for the release of Armando Pascal.
Julianne left Shayne's office to inform old Pascal about his son's impending release.
Shayne married Mademoiselle de Angie.
Blaire obtained a recommendation from Julianne to a Spanish merchant.
Abrielle expressed concern for Quinton's safety.
Julianne paid the expenses of old Pascal's funeral.
Pascal urged the inspector to reopen the case.
Pascal stood still and meditated quietly.
Pascal resolved to cast out his meals from his cell window.
Pascal heard a scratching noise coming from the wall of his cell.
Pascal drank the soup brought by the jailer and regained strength.
Pascal devoured the fresh provisions brought by the jailer.
Pascal shifted aside from the window upon hearing a rustling sound.
Pascal concealed the sharp fragments of the broken jug in his bed.
